name: Weight Watcher's Irish Stew
recipeIngredient: 1 pound (450 grams) lean boneless lamb, cut into 1.5-inch squares
6 large new red potatoes, peeled and quartered
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large heavy saucepan, heat 1/2 of the oil over medium-high heat.
Add 1/2 of the lamb or beef and cook until well browned on one side, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir and cook for an additional minute; then remove meat and set aside.
2. Add the remaining oil to the pan with the lamb (or beef) and chopped onion.
Cook until browned, approximately 8-10 minutes. Return the first batch of meat to the pan.
3. Add broth and bring mixture to a boil over medium heat.
Add thyme and bay leaf; lower heat to low and simmer for 1 hour.
4. Add potatoes, onions, ale, and salt. Cover and continue simmering until potatoes are tender, about 30-40 minutes.
5. To thicken the stew, mix cornstarch with 1/4 cup cold water in a small bowl until smooth.
Stir this mixture into the simmering stew; cook gently until thickened.
6. Garnish with parsley and serve alongside bread.
Serving (with one slice of bread) provides:
- Half vegetable, 3 proteins, 2 breads: 37 calories
Per serving: With bread: 409 calories, 29 grams protein, 9 grams fat, 644 mg sodium.
Without bread: 332 calories, 27 grams protein, 8 grams fat, 478 mg sodium.
